channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: American college student believed to have drowned in big wave: report
item.description: Sudiksha Konanki, an American college student who vanished in the Dominican Republic during a spring break trip, is believed to have drowned in the ocean.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump loves the Gilded Age and its tariffs. It was a great time for the rich but not for the many
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In President Donald Trump's idealized framing, the United States was at its zenith in the 1890s, when top hats and shirtwaists were fashionable and typhoid fever often killed more soldiers than combat.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: DOGE blows the lid off massive number of loans granted to kids and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI made its way to vineyards. Here's how the technology is helping make your wine
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When artificial intelligence-backed tractors became available to vineyards, Tom Gamble wanted to be an early adopter. He knew there would be a learning curve, but Gamble decided the technology was worth figuring out. The third-generation farmer bought one autonomous tractor.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans worry Trump's tariffs could harm economy
item.description: Republican lawmakers are growing alarmed over signs that President Trump’s expanding trade war is hurting the economy, something they’re hearing from constituents at home who are struggling to adapt to Trump’s zigzagging tariff pronouncements.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Measles case confirmed in Maryland resident who recently travelled abroad
item.description: Early symptoms include a fever higher than 101 degrees, a runny nose, cough and red, watery eyes. A person will experience a red rash one to four days after early symptoms.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope following Vatican's spiritual retreat from afar as he recovers in hospital
item.description: On Thursday, the Vatican will mark the 12th anniversary of Francis’ election, the first with the pope out of sight but still very much in charge.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICE forced to release some illegal migrants who could pose danger to Americans: immigration attorney
item.description: Despite ICE highlighting an increase in illegal immigrant arrests during President Donald Trump's administration, the agency is facing challenges with limited detention space.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bryan Kohberger defense ramps up legal maneuvers in bid to keep Idaho murders suspect off death row
item.description: Bryan Kohberger wants to stop Idaho prosecutors from discussing "murder," "bushy eyebrows" and psychopaths in his upcoming student murders trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tokyo was filled with charred corpses after US firebombing 80 years ago. Survivors want compensation
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — More than 100,000 people were killed in a single night 80 years ago Monday in the U.S. firebombing of Tokyo, the Japanese capital. The attack, made with conventional bombs, destroyed downtown Tokyo and filled the streets with heaps of charred bodies.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI and MAYUKO ONO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Christopher Bell holds off Denny Hamlin at Phoenix to win third straight NASCAR Cup Series race
item.description: Bell survived a late drag race with Hamlin to become the first NASCAR Cup Series driver to win three straight races in the NextGen car, edging his teammate by 0.049 seconds to win the second-closest race in Phoenix Raceway history on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ukrainian war veteran amputees learn adaptive skiing in Oregon
item.description: HOODOO SKI AREA, Ore. (AP) — Ukrainian war veteran Oleksandr Shvachka lost his left leg to Russian tank fire outside Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH and JENNY KANE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Drawing huge crowds, Bernie Sanders steps into leadership of the anti-Trump resistance
item.description: WARREN, Mich. (AP) — Bernie Sanders is standing alone on the back of a pickup truck shouting into a bullhorn. He's facing several hundred ecstatic voters huddled outside a suburban Detroit high school — the group that did not fit inside the high school's gym or two overflow rooms.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: An Arizona prisoner whose execution is coming up isn't asking for a reprieve
item.description: A prisoner scheduled to be executed in what would be Arizona’s first use of the death penalty in over two years will not ask for a reprieve from his death sentence.
item.dc:creator: Jacques Billeaud, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Immigration agents arrest Palestinian activist who helped lead Columbia University protests
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal immigration authorities arrested a Palestinian activist Saturday who played a prominent role in Columbia University’s protests against Israel, a significant escalation in the Trump administration’s pledge to detain and deport student activists.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scholars stranded in America and abroad amid funding freeze of State Department programs
item.description: Fulbright scholar Aubrey Lay was supposed to get paid for three months of work by the U.S. government through his teaching assistantship at a school for Ukrainian refugees in Estonia. Instead, he only got about one week's pay and no word on when he might see the rest of his grant.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NORAD intercepts aircraft flying in restricted zone above Mar-a-Lago in Florida
item.description: Military officials detected a civilian aircraft that breached a temporary flight restriction near President Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago estate and escorted it out.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Measles case reported at Washington Dulles International Airport in Virginia
item.description: The Virginia Department of Health was notified of a confirmed case of measles at Washington Dulles International Airport on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Chrysnel Banzouzi, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pushes to shut down the Dept. of Education: What impact it has on Florida
item.description: Florida relies on billions in Federal education funding, from special education to student loan programs.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Amazon driver sexually assaulted woman, girl making deliveries: Police
item.description: An Amazon delivery driver has been charged after allegedly sexually assaulting a woman and girl in the Los Angeles area in two separate instances, Glendale police said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Laura Morrison, NewsNation

item.title: Retired NYPD cop drains savings to help son fight rare brain cancer
item.description: A year ago, Derek Dym was diagnosed with DMG, an aggressive brain cancer affecting just 300 children annually.
item.dc:creator: Richard Bodee, NewsNation

item.title: A single-engine plane crashes near a Pennsylvania airport and all 5 aboard are taken to hospitals
item.description: A single-engine airplane carrying five people crashed and burst into flames Sunday in the parking lot of a retirement community near a small airport in suburban Pennsylvania, and everyone on board survived, officials and witnesses said. The fiery crash happened around 3 p.m.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge awards nearly $2M after concluding FBI agent was negligent in shooting kidnapped Texas man
item.description: A federal judge has awarded nearly $2 million in damages as part of a civil lawsuit after concluding an FBI agent was negligent when he fatally shot a kidnapped Texas man during a botched rescue attempt in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Jersey police officer killed, second officer wounded in shooting by 14-year-old, authorities say
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — A 14-year-old shot and killed a police officer and wounded another Friday evening in New Jersey, authorities said, in a chaotic scene that witnesses described as seeing officers running down a busy street before they heard a volley of a dozen or more gunshots.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy arrested for assault in Texas
item.description: On Friday, Chiefs star rookie Xavier Worthy, 21, was arrested in Williamson County, Texas after investigators said he assaulted a family member or person in his household by "impeding their breathing or circulation.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Why Chi-Chi’s restaurants are returning 2 decades after their closure
item.description: While multiple casual dining chains are looking to reduce their footprints this year, a once-beloved but long-shuttered brand is preparing for a comeback.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pickleball isn't named after a dog: How the sport really earned its moniker
item.description: You may have heard a rumor about the game being named after a dog. Spoiler alert: That isn't the case.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: State Dept using AI to ban visas over perceived pro-Hamas posts
item.description: The initiative, started under Secretary of State Marco Rubio, will use AI to scan student visa holders’ social media accounts.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, ABC 4

item.title: A South Carolina man executed by firing squad is the first US prisoner killed this way in 15 years
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat was executed by firing squad Friday, the first U.S. prisoner in 15 years to die by that method, which he saw as preferable to the electric chair or lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A car pulled from a river may tell what happened to an Oregon family of 5 that went missing in 1958
item.description: After two days of dredging, a crane on Friday pulled from the Columbia River a Ford station wagon that officials believe belonged to an Oregon family who disappeared while on a trip 66 years ago to find Christmas greenery.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A timeline of how actor Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa died at their New Mexico home
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — Actor Gene Hackman died of heart disease a full week after his wife died from hantavirus in their New Mexico home, likely unaware that she was dead because he was in the advanced stages of Alzheimer’s disease, authorities revealed Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CDC launches 'conflicts of interest' page about vaccine advisory panel
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) published a page on Friday listing the conflicts of interest reported by members of a key vaccine advisory committee.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Reopening Texas detention centers to hold deportees worries migrant advocates
item.description: Two controversial immigration detention facilities in South Texas are being reopened for the detention and deportation of migrant women and families, officials tell Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: 20 Dem AGs sue over Trump efforts to fire probationary employees
item.description: Democratic state attorneys general in Washington, D.C., and 19 states, including California, sued the Trump administration late Thursday over its efforts to carry out mass firings of federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Family on cruise ship gets lost luggage back after weeks of waiting, but half of their personal items are missing
item.description: Frustrations continue for Jason and Tammy Merritt after waiting a month for their lost luggage, which ended up being on-board the Carnival Cruise Line the entire time.
item.dc:creator: Mecca Thompson/KFOR, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Walgreens set to go private in deal worth nearly $10 billion
item.description: Walgreens Boots Alliance says it has agreed to be acquired by private equity firm Sycamore Partners as the struggling retailer looks to turn itself around after years of losing money.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Police identify 2 people who died in Feb. 19 midair collision at southern Arizona airport
item.description: MARANA, Ariz. (AP) — Authorities have publicly identified two people killed two weeks ago in the midair collision of two small planes at a southern Arizona airport. Police say 70-year-old Michael Reinath and 76-year-old Linda Gifford died in the Feb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
